front: Photograph: shows a nude John Lennon curled in a fetal position around his fully clothed wife Yoko Ono, taken hours before Lennon was murdered
back: John Lennon and Yoko Ono (Annie Leibovitz)

front: Artist: Sam Taylor-Wood and Henry Bond recreated her most famous portrait in the work '26 October 1993'
Photograph: 1991 Vanity Fair cover depicting a seven-months-pregnant, nude subject holding her belly
back: Demi Moore Vanity Fair cover (Annie Leibovitz)

front: Photograph: 2015 Vanity Fair cover showing the subject in a white dress with the caption 'Call Me Caitlyn'
Photograph: shows an overturned bicycle next to a trail and pool of blood on a Sarajevo street, taken during the Bosnian War
Artist: traveled to Sarajevo and documented the Bosnian War while accompanied by partner Susan Sontag
Artist: her long-term partner was writer Susan Sontag, whom she also photographed in a bear suit
Artist: emulated Margaret Bourke-White by mounting the eagle gargoyle of the Chrysler Building to photograph dancer David Parsons
Artist: photographed Whoopi Goldberg submerged in a bathtub full of milk
Artist: photographed a nude Keith Haring standing atop a coffee table covered in black-and-white paint, blending into his similarly patterned living room
Artist: climbed Mount Fuji after buying her first camera (a Minolta SR-T 101), as recounted in the memoir At Work
Artist: photographed Julia Margaret Cameron's cottage in the book Pilgrimage
